# False-positive corpus for ClipWarden classifier
#
# Every non-blank, non-comment line in this file MUST classify as None.
# A regression here means something non-address is being treated as an
# address. Given the threat model (arming a detector that will later
# warn on substitution), an FP here is worse than a missed classify:
# it turns the tool into noise.
#
# Categories are grouped together rather than alphabetised so the next
# reader can tell what each block is testing at a glance.
#
# The synthetic adversarial entries at the bottom are produced by
# tools/gen_fixtures.py and pasted in. Re-run the generator if you
# want to refresh them; the seed is fixed so the output is stable.

# GIT SHAs (real, from my other repos)
04c3614c5dbe345edb7bd22df1ac46b93e08154b
a403106e1db52509bdc98779aab08bd626839867
490f00a67ace14a6a1ff528f677ffcdf5e8a2b0d
1752df4015d34d36f163edbaec8422701c1ef974
5dfef352e5b033ea90bc79c4f121c7db5f12ee1c
b48483735d621f4f507d517bd0ff14848b645205
7bba1407ed2c7e5000117a507590f36a122024f6
530a741828dd9891b9a2609eca2d1c1bd26f383e
b9aaf0c32fea6f3c9d5fb0c09aba920a0ac49256
88c9fce7c2f894e7757faa0df567ad843e16f59e
04c3614
a403106
490f00a

# STRIPE TOKENS (synthetic, deliberately broken so push-protection
# scanners don't mistake them for real keys; the classifier only cares
# that these aren't crypto addresses, which still holds.)
sk_fake_ISUqgFvGkbfI2pfRfrx6tfPD
